q: Among the thinkers in this hub, who argues most directly against Stoic detachment, and on what grounds? Ground it in a corpus.
final:
Among the thinkers surfaced in this hub, the clearest candidate for arguing against Stoic detachment is **Arthur Schopenhauer**.

What I can ground from the retrieved corpus is limited, but the Schopenhauer hub explicitly frames him as a **“practical philosophy”** thinker whose knowledge base is devoted to his late works, **“Мудрость жизни”** and **“Наставления и максимы”** (*Aphorisms of Worldly Wisdom*), i.e. a corpus centered on living wisely rather than on Stoic-style emotional distance. The hub description says:

> “База знаний посвящена практической философии Артура Шопенгауэра, изложенной в его поздних работах «Мудрость жизни» и «Наставления и максимы» (*Aphorismen ...*)”

Source: https://trip2g.com/ru/hub/schopenhauer

That said, I **cannot yet substantiate the “against Stoic detachment” claim directly from the retrieved text**, because the section read was only the note header, and it does **not** explicitly discuss detachment, Stoicism, compassion, or the grounds of any criticism.

So the best corpus-grounded answer is:

- **Most likely thinker:** Schopenhauer
- **Why this one:** the hub identifies him as a practical philosopher whose corpus is organized around worldly wisdom and maxims, which is the closest retrieved evidence to an anti-detachment stance
- **What I cannot confirm from the retrieved material:** the exact objection to Stoic detachment and its grounds

If you want, I can continue by reading the Schopenhauer sections that may actually mention **compassion, suffering, or rejection of Stoic apathy** and then give a fully grounded comparison against Stoicism.
